headline: Retail investor buying holds strong: Why these names are winning
summary: Amid heightened geopolitical volatility, retail investors have been rotating back into defense stock and tried-and-true names, like Nvidia (NVDA) and Microsoft (MSFT). Yahoo Finance Senior Business Reporter Ines Ferré and B. Riley Wealth chief market strategist Art Hogan join Opening Bid host Brian Sozzi to discuss recent retail investing trends. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Opening Bid.

headline: 3 Reasons RTX is Risky and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
summary: Since March 2021, the S&P 500 has delivered a total return of 79.5%. But one standout stock has more than doubled the market - over the past five years, RTX has surged 170% to $204.06 per share. Its momentum hasn’t stopped as it’s also gained 32.3% in the last six months thanks to its solid quarterly results, beating the S&P by 26.7%.

content.title: 3 Reasons RTX is Risky and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
content.summary: Since March 2021, the S&P 500 has delivered a total return of 79.5%. But one standout stock has more than doubled the market - over the past five years, RTX has surged 170% to $204.06 per share. Its momentum hasn’t stopped as it’s also gained 32.3% in the last six months thanks to its solid quarterly results, beating the S&P by 26.7%.

headline: Trump Says Defense Firms to Quadruple Output of Some Weapons
summary: “We just concluded a very good meeting with the largest U.S. Defense Manufacturing Companies where we discussed Production and Production Schedules,” Trump said in a social media post on Friday.  “They have agreed to quadruple Production of the ‘Exquisite Class’ Weaponry in that we want to reach, as rapidly as possible, the highest levels of quantity.”

content.title: Trump Says Defense Firms to Quadruple Output of Some Weapons
content.summary: “We just concluded a very good meeting with the largest U.S. Defense Manufacturing Companies where we discussed Production and Production Schedules,” Trump said in a social media post on Friday.  “They have agreed to quadruple Production of the ‘Exquisite Class’ Weaponry in that we want to reach, as rapidly as possible, the highest levels of quantity.”

headline: Trump Says Weapons Makers Agree to Expand Production
summary: President Trump said America’s largest weapons makers have agreed to raise production of military munitions following “a very good meeting” at the White House on Friday.  Executives from BAE Systems, Boeing, Honeywell Aerospace, L3Harris Technologies, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman and RTX attended Friday’s meeting, Trump said.  “They have agreed to quadruple Production of the ‘Exquisite Class’ Weaponry in that we want to reach, as rapidly as possible, the highest levels of quantity,” Trump wrote in a social media post.

content.title: Trump Says Weapons Makers Agree to Expand Production
content.summary: President Trump said America’s largest weapons makers have agreed to raise production of military munitions following “a very good meeting” at the White House on Friday.  Executives from BAE Systems, Boeing, Honeywell Aerospace, L3Harris Technologies, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman and RTX attended Friday’s meeting, Trump said.  “They have agreed to quadruple Production of the ‘Exquisite Class’ Weaponry in that we want to reach, as rapidly as possible, the highest levels of quantity,” Trump wrote in a social media post.
